title: pleioceras afzelii
intro: Pleioceras afzelii is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the 'African Green Tea' due to its resemblance to Camellia sinensis, the tea plant. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to have various health benefits. The plant is said to help in reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, among other benefits. In herbal preparations, Pleioceras afzelii is often used as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be consumed raw or dried for later use.
